title: Monetary Policy Report—July 2024
summary: The Bank of Canada reduced its target for the overnight rate by 25 basis points to 4.5% in July 2024, with the Bank Rate at 4.75% and deposit rate at 4.5%. The Bank continued its policy of balance sheet normalization while noting that CPI inflation is moving closer to the 2% target and core inflation has eased significantly. Core inflation is expected to ease to about 2.5% in the second half of 2024, with CPI projected to return sustainably to 2% later in 2025.
key_points: Bank of Canada reduced policy rate by 25 bps to 4.5% target overnight rate; Bank Rate at 4.75%, deposit rate at 4.5%
Bank continuing policy of balance sheet normalization
CPI inflation is moving closer to the 2% target and is no longer broad-based; core inflation measures have eased significantly
Core inflation expected to ease to approximately 2.5% in H2 2024, then continue gradual decline
CPI inflation forecast to temporarily fall below core inflation (due to gasoline prices) before rising modestly into H1 2025

Risks to inflation outlook are considered balanced; upside risks include persistent services price inflation and geopolitical tensions; downside risks include weaker household spending and slower global growth
why_it_matters: This quarterly Monetary Policy Report provides the official outlook for the Canadian economy and signals the Bank of Canada's easing trajectory. The 25 bps rate cut marks the beginning of an easing cycle and reflects the Bank's view that inflation is returning to target. Market participants use these projections for CAD positioning, bond yields, and Canadian equity valuations. The balanced risk assessment suggests no immediate urgency for either tighter or looser policy.
macro_context: Canada's economy expected to strengthen in H2 2024 and into 2025. Export growth anticipated to pick up and household spending should strengthen as borrowing costs decline. Population growth projected to slow due to new limits on temporary resident arrivals. US economic growth has slowed after surprising strength; euro area expanding after 2023 stalling. Global economy expected to grow at around 3% over projection horizon. Elevated wage growth and persistent services inflation remain concerns.
market_structure_context: The Bank mentions continuing balance sheet normalization but provides no specific details on pace or holdings in this summary content.

human_review.notes: Historical record from July 2024; content quality is high from Bank of Canada but the crawled content is the overview/landing page rather than full report. Consider retrieving PDF for complete projections and detailed analysis.

notes: This is a historical Bank of Canada Monetary Policy Report from July 2024. The source content consists of the web page overview/navigation rather than the full report text.
